title: Utricularia macrocheilos
taxonomy: Utricularia macrocheilos is classified under the family Lentibulariaceae. This plant belongs to the order Lamiales. It is a species of bladderwort, a type of carnivorous plant. The classification of Utricularia macrocheilos is further categorized as belonging to the genus Utricularia. It is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique carnivorous characteristics.
uses: Utricularia macrocheilos has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. In some Asian cultures, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Utricularia macrocheilos for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.

intro: Utricularia macrocheilos, commonly known as the large-leaved bladderwort, is a carnivorous perennial plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting small aquatic organisms. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Utricularia macrocheilos include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and teas, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
